creator: stefan@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hello Tomek,

Tomek CEDRO, 17.05.10, 21:37h CEST:

> The problem I have reported was about binary urjtag using libftdi
> 0.14, when 0.17 is already out. Also there are some problems with
> building the port. Below is the output. I am using the fresh ports
> tree.
> 
> When trying to pkg_add the binary package it complains that another
> version of the libftdi is required, also it won't run because of the
> library mismatch. If it is possible please update the binary according
> to the libftdi version.
> 
> # pkg_add -r urjtag
> Fetching ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/i386/packages-8.0-release/Latest/urjtag.tbz...
> Done.
> pkg_add: warning: package 'urjtag-0.10' requires 'libftdi-0.14_1', but
> 'libftdi-0.17' is installed
> # jtag
> /libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Shared object "libftdi.so.15" not found,
> required by "jtag"

Looking at the URL above, I understand that you're installing binary
packages for 8.0-RELEASE while you have already built and installed ports
from a much more recent ports tree. The shared library version of libftdi
has changed in the meantime, so that can't work. (urjtag in the package
you installed is linked against an older version of libftdi.)

> When I tried to build the urjtag from the ports, still, it gives me
> the build error:

Given the above situation and the fact that it works for others, I would
suggest that you rebuild at least all dependencies of devel/urjtag from
ports or stick to the binary packages (which have been built to work
together).

Regards,
Stefan
